project.name: CargoFin_Backend
project.description: Trade finance platform for DP World — handles credit facilities, finance applications, lending, insurance, compliance, and more

conventions.routePattern: Plain objects with method, path, function properties exported from route files. NOT app.get() style.
conventions.controllerPattern: module.exports = { fnName: async (req, res) => { try { ... } catch (err) { respondError(res, err); } } }
conventions.errorHandling: try/catch in controllers → respondError(res, err). Custom error classes: APIError, ArgumentError, JoiSchemaError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, UserAuthenticationError

APIError.use: External API failures (Oracle, Service Bus, third-party)

ArgumentError.use: Invalid function arguments in service layer

conventions.validation: Joi schemas in /schemas directories. Validated via joiValidation.validate(schema, payload)
conventions.logging: logger.info(), logger.error(), logger.warn() from @utils/logger. NEVER console.log in production.
conventions.slackNotifications: slackNotify.info/error() for critical operations and errors
conventions.database: Knex query builder — config.getDb() returns db instance. Transactions via db.transaction(trx => ...)

transactionPattern.when: Any operation that writes to multiple tables
transactionPattern.rule: Pass trx to all db_service functions inside the transaction, not db

responseFormat.error: respondError(res, err) — automatically formats error response
responseFormat.list: { success: true, data: [...], pagination: { total, page, limit } }

permissionPattern.location: Defined in permissions table, attached to routes via permission property
permissionPattern.check: Middleware checks user permissions before controller executes

conventions.strictMode: 'use strict' at top of every file
conventions.jsdoc: JSDoc comment on every exported function

layerResponsibilities.routes: Define HTTP endpoints only. Map method + path to controller function. Attach middleware and permissions. Never contain business logic.
layerResponsibilities.controllers: Parse request (req.body, req.params, req.query, req.headers). Validate with Joi schema. Call service layer. Format HTTP response. Handle errors via respondError(res, err).
layerResponsibilities.services: Contain all business logic. Call db_service for data access. Call external_services for APIs. Orchestrate workflows. Never access req/res directly.
layerResponsibilities.db_services: Raw database queries only using Knex. Accept (db/trx, params). Return data. No business logic, no HTTP awareness.
layerResponsibilities.middleware: Cross-cutting concerns: auth, rate limiting, user context, request parsing.
layerResponsibilities.schemas: Joi validation schemas. Export a single schema object per file.
layerResponsibilities.migrations: Database schema changes. Always create NEW migration files, never modify existing ones. Must be rollback-safe.

workflowSystem.description: Feature workflows are state machines managed via 3 components

tables.lending.workflow: Defines state machine — maps (workflow_type, state, action) → (next_state, active_user, allowed_services). Each row is a state transition.
tables.lending.skip_workflow: Defines conditions where a workflow step can be auto-skipped. Maps (workflow_type, skip_condition) → action.
tables.config WORKFLOW_TYPE: Constants that identify workflow types. Defined in config/product_config.

workflowSystem.pattern: New workflows require: (1) new WORKFLOW_TYPE constant, (2) rows in lending.workflow for each state transition, (3) rows in lending.skip_workflow for skip conditions, (4) processWorkflow() call in the service layer
workflowSystem.key_function: processWorkflow(trx, entityId, workflow, workType, action, notes, external, workflowState, changeActive, processTogether, notificationSend, requestId, skipParamsMap)

serviceBus.description: Azure Service Bus for async inter-service communication
serviceBus.pattern: Messages have eventType, eventSubType, payload. Consumers use handleServiceEvent to process.

oracleFusion.description: Oracle ERP integration for accounting (AR, receipts, credit notes, interest accrual)

services.oracle_fusion.js: Main Oracle integration — tax, exchange rates, bank details, interest accrual
services.oracle_credit_note_service.js: Credit memo/note creation via Oracle API with retry and response handling

oracleFusion.pattern: Call Oracle API → handle response → update local status. On failure: move to Resubmission state, allow retry.

notificationSystem.key_function: sendNotification(trx, usersOrgsToNotify, ownerId, ownerType, messageToBeSent, type, actionable, notificationType, markPreviousNotificationsRead, ccEmails, notificationInfo)
notificationSystem.email_function: sendEmailNotification(trx, usersToEmail, ownerId, ownerType, emailType, message, subject, attachments, ccEmails)
notificationSystem.recipient_resolution: getUserDeptListForEmail() from process_workflow/helper.js resolves role-based recipients

departmentTypes.description: User roles/departments in the workflow system

rules: Always produce the required deliverable at each checkpoint
Always STOP and wait for explicit human approval before proceeding
If human requests changes, incorporate them and re-present the checkpoint
Track checkpoint status: PENDING → APPROVED → (next checkpoint)
Modules can be implemented in parallel if they have no dependencies
Each module should be completable in 1-2 conversation sessions
State module transitions explicitly: 'Starting Module N' / 'Module N Complete'

when_starting_module: Read Context Bootstrap files from module spec

testing.mockStrategy: jest.mock for external dependencies and services
